title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: vignette walks create_*() functions build PK-Sim Building blocks named arguments, add_*() / remove_*() mutators attach snapshot. use Midazolam template throughout:

title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: create_individual() builds Individual demographic arguments. PK-Sim seeds underlying physiological tree simulation time. Extra demographics (e.g. gestational_age) accepted additional named arguments: Attach result snapshot add_individual(): Parameters sit specific path inside individual’s physiological tree built create_parameter() using path argument. PK-Sim writes path pipe-separated segments (Organism|Liver|...): can pass list parameters straight create_individual(parameters = ...), assign individual$parameters fact (see “Modifying existing building blocks” ).
code: patient <- create_individual(   name = "Patient_001",   species = "Human",   population = "European_ICRP_2002",   gender = "FEMALE",   age = 35,   weight = 65,   height = 165 )  patient #>  #> ── Individual: Patient_001 ───────────────────────────────────────────────────── #>  #> ── Characteristics ── #>  #> • Species: Human #> • Population: European_ICRP_2002 #> • Gender: FEMALE #> • Age: 35 year(s) #> • Height: 165 cm #> • Weight: 65 kg pregnant_patient <- create_individual(   name = "Pregnant_Patient",   species = "Human",   population = "European_ICRP_2002",   gender = "FEMALE",   age = 28,   weight = 70,   height = 168,   gestational_age = 20 # weeks )  pregnant_patient #>  #> ── Individual: Pregnant_Patient ──────────────────────────────────────────────── #>  #> ── Characteristics ── #>  #> • Species: Human #> • Population: European_ICRP_2002 #> • Gender: FEMALE #> • Age: 28 year(s) #> • Gestational Age: 20 week(s) #> • Height: 168 cm #> • Weight: 70 kg snapshot <- add_individual(snapshot, patient) snapshot$individuals #>  #> ── Individuals (3) ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> • European (P-gp modified, CYP3A4 36 h) #> • Korean (Yu 2004 study) #> • Patient_001 ehc <- create_parameter(   path = "Organism|Liver|EHC continuous fraction",   value = 0.4 )  gfr <- create_parameter(   path = "Organism|Kidney|GFR",   value = 120,   unit = "ml/min",   source = "Literature",   description = "Normal adult GFR",   source_id = 123 )  gfr #>  #> ── Parameter: Organism|Kidney|GFR  #> • Value: 120 #> • Unit: ml/min #> • Source: Literature #> • Description: Normal adult GFR

title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: create_formulation() covers every PK-Sim release profile ("Dissolved", "Weibull", "First Order", "Zero Order", "Lint80", "Particles", "Table"). accepted parameters depend chosen type: "Dissolved" takes none; kinetic profiles ("Weibull", "First Order", "Zero Order", "Lint80", "Particles", "Table") accept release-kinetics parameters specific profile (example "Weibull" formulation accepts dissolution_time, dissolution_shape, lag_time, …). See ?create_formulation parameter list per type.
code: oral_solution <- create_formulation(   name = "Oral Solution 10mg/mL",   type = "Dissolved" )  oral_solution #>  #> ── Formulation: Oral Solution 10mg/mL ────────────────────────────────────────── #> • Type: Dissolved  tablet <- create_formulation(   name = "Immediate Release Tablet",   type = "Weibull",   parameters = list(     dissolution_time = 30,     dissolution_time_unit = "min",     lag_time = 5,     lag_time_unit = "min",     dissolution_shape = 1.2,     suspension = TRUE   ) )  tablet #>  #> ── Formulation: Immediate Release Tablet ─────────────────────────────────────── #> • Type: Weibull #>  #> ── Parameters ── #>  #> • Dissolution time (50% dissolved): 30 min #> • Lag time: 5 min #> • Dissolution shape: 1.2 #> • Use as suspension: 1 snapshot <- add_formulation(snapshot, oral_solution) snapshot <- add_formulation(snapshot, tablet)

title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: create_compound() builds compound validation common fields molecular_weight_unit. Compound-level parameters (clearances, halogens, …) passed list create_parameter() results: parameters named compound identified name, omit path argument.
code: drug <- create_compound(name = "Drug X")  drug <- create_compound(   name = "Drug X",   is_small_molecule = TRUE,   molecular_weight = 250.3,   plasma_protein_binding_partner = "Albumin",   parameters = list(     create_parameter(name = "Cl_spec", value = 5, unit = "ml/min/kg")   ) )  drug #>  #> ── Compound: Drug X ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #>  #> ── Basic Properties ── #>  #> • Type: Small Molecule #> • Plasma Protein Binding Partner: Albumin #> • Molecular Weight: 250.3 g/mol #>  #> ── Physicochemical Properties ── #>  #> ── Additional Parameters ── #>  #> • Additional Parameters (1 total): #>   • Cl_spec: 5 ml/min/kg [Unknown]

title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: create_process() builds compound process (plasma protein binding, metabolism, clearance, …) named arguments. internal_name identifies process template PK-Sim’s compound process repository (example "SpecificBinding", "MetabolizationSpecific_MM", "GlomerularFiltration"); data_source labels data origin. See ?create_process usage current examples; canonical list internal_name values maintained PK-Sim groups categories surfaced process$category (protein_binding_partners, metabolizing_enzymes, hepatic_clearance, transporter_proteins, renal_clearance, biliary_clearance, inhibition, induction). $category active binding derives domain label ("protein_binding_partners", "metabolizing_enzymes", "hepatic_clearance", "transporter_proteins", "renal_clearance", "biliary_clearance", "inhibition", "induction") internal_name. Compound processes loaded snapshot exposed flat named list Process objects via compound$processes, long-form processes tibble returned get_compounds_dfs() surfaces every (process, parameter) pair row.
code: binding <- create_process(   internal_name = "SpecificBinding",   data_source = "Buhr 1997",   molecule = "GABRG2" )  metabolism <- create_process(   internal_name = "MetabolizationSpecific_MM",   data_source = "Optimized",   molecule = "CYP3A4",   metabolite = "Hydroxy-Drug",   parameters = list(     create_parameter(name = "Km", value = 1.2, unit = "µmol/l"),     create_parameter(name = "kcat", value = 9.865, unit = "1/min")   ) )  metabolism$category #> [1] "metabolizing_enzymes"

title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: create_population() builds Population recipe: settings used PK-Sim sample cohort simulation time. Use range() age, weight, height, BMI bounds.
code: adults <- create_population(   name = "Healthy Adults",   number_of_individuals = 50,   proportion_of_females = 50,   species = "Human",   source_population = "European_ICRP_2002",   age_range = range(20, 60, "year(s)"),   weight_range = range(50, 90, "kg") )  adults #>  #> ── Population: Healthy Adults ── #>  #> Source Population: European_ICRP_2002 #> Number of individuals: 50 #> Proportion of females: 50% #> Age range: 20 - 60 year(s) #> Weight range: 50 - 90 kg

title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: create_expression_profile() builds ExpressionProfile. identity profile composite Molecule|Species|Category, three required, along molecule type.
code: cyp3a4 <- create_expression_profile(   molecule = "CYP3A4",   species = "Human",   category = "Healthy",   type = "Enzyme",   ontogeny = "CYP3A4" )  cyp3a4 #>  #> ── Expression Profile: CYP3A4 (Enzyme) ───────────────────────────────────────── #> • Species: Human #> • Category: Healthy #> • Ontogeny: CYP3A4

title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: create_protocol() builds Protocol. default creates Simple Protocol (one application type, one dose, optional dosing interval); pass schemas create Advanced Protocol.
code: single_dose <- create_protocol(   name = "Single dose 10mg",   application_type = "Oral",   dosing_interval = "Single",   parameters = list(     create_parameter(name = "Start time", value = 0, unit = "h"),     create_parameter(name = "InputDose", value = 10, unit = "mg")   ) )  single_dose #>  #> ── Protocol: Single dose 10mg ────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> • Type: Simple #> • Application Type: Oral #> • Dosing Interval: Once #>  #> ── Parameters ── #>  #> • Start time: 0 h #> • InputDose: 10 mg

title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: dosing schedules fit single application (multi-day course, two compounds dosed staggered schedules), use Advanced Protocol. Advanced Protocol built one Schemas; schema describes repeating block applications (example “10 mg orally every 24 hours 5 days”). Inside schema, individual application SchemaItem. build create_schema() (repeating block) create_schema_item() (one application), pass list schemas create_protocol(): application_type schema item validated canonical PK-Sim application types ("Oral", "IntravenousBolus", "IntravenousInfusion", "Intramuscular", "Subcutaneous", "Dermal", "Rectal", "Inhalation", "Intraperitoneal").
code: qd_oral <- create_protocol(   name = "Once daily oral, 5 days",   schemas = list(     create_schema(       name = "Schema 1",       parameters = list(         create_parameter(name = "NumberOfRepetitions", value = 5),         create_parameter(name = "TimeBetweenRepetitions", value = 24, unit = "h")       ),       items = list(         create_schema_item(           name = "Item 1",           application_type = "Oral",           parameters = list(             create_parameter(name = "Start time", value = 0, unit = "h"),             create_parameter(name = "InputDose", value = 10, unit = "mg")           )         )       )     )   ),   time_unit = "h" )  qd_oral #>  #> ── Protocol: Once daily oral, 5 days ─────────────────────────────────────────── #> • Type: Advanced (Schema-based) #> • Time Unit: h #>  #> ── Schemas ── #>  #> • Schema: Schema 1 #>   • Item 1: Oral -

title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: Event discrete perturbation drug administration: meal, change posture, enzyme inducer dose, exercise bout. create_event() builds one template (name event recipe PK-Sim’s event database) plus optional parameter overrides. PK-Sim clones named template simulation time applies overrides. set available templates defined inside PK-Sim (particular PK-Sim Events database ships installation), exact list valid template names depends PK-Sim setup. Templates commonly seen published snapshots include “Meal: Standard (Human)” family food effects; need template installation, define PK-Sim first re-export.
code: breakfast <- create_event(   name = "Breakfast",   template = "Meal: Standard (Human)",   parameters = list(     create_parameter(name = "Meal energy content", value = 500, unit = "kcal"),     create_parameter(name = "Meal volume", value = 0.3, unit = "l")   ) )  breakfast #>  #> ── Event: Breakfast ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> • Template: Meal: Standard (Human) #>  #> ── Parameters: ── #>  #> • Meal energy content: 500 kcal #> • Meal volume: 0.3 l

title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: Observer simulation-time formula exposes derived quantity (example tissue concentration) natural model output. Observers travel groups called Observer sets. create_observer_set() builds set name list observers. Build observer Observer$new() (validates input gives typed accessors $name, $type, $dimension, $formula (full ExplicitFormula list), $formula_expression, $formula_dimension, $formula_references, $container_tags), bundle set attach set snapshot:
code: brain_obs <- Observer$new(list(   Name = "brain_plasma_conc",   Type = "Container",   Dimension = "Concentration (molar)",   Formula = list(Formula = "Conc_Br") ))  brain_set <- create_observer_set(   name = "BrainPlasmaConcentration",   observers = list(brain_obs) )  brain_set #>  #> ── ObserverSet: BrainPlasmaConcentration ─────────────────────────────────────── #> • 1 observer  snapshot <- add_observer_set(snapshot, brain_set)

title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: create_observed_data() builds ospsuite::DataSet named arguments time grid, measurement values, units, optional error series. value_dimension required gates unit validation.
code: obs <- create_observed_data(   name = "Phase I - Subject 001",   time = c(0, 0.5, 1, 2, 4, 8, 12, 24),   values = c(0, 12.5, 18.2, 15.8, 11.2, 6.8, 3.4, 1.1),   time_unit = "h",   value_unit = "mg/l",   value_dimension = "Concentration (mass)" )  obs #> <DataSet> #>   • Name: Phase I - Subject 001 #>   • X dimension: Time #>   • X unit: h #>   • Y dimension: Concentration (mass) #>   • Y unit: mg/l #>   • Error type: NULL #>   • Error unit: NULL #>   • Molecular weight: NULL #>   • LLOQ: NULL #> Meta data:

title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: building-block kind paired add_*() remove_*() mutators take snapshot first return invisibly, chain base pipe. can pass several names remove :
code: snapshot <- remove_individual(snapshot, "Patient_001") snapshot <- remove_formulation(snapshot, "Oral Solution 10mg/mL") snapshot <- remove_individual(snapshot, c("Patient_001", "Patient_002")) snapshot <- remove_expression_profile(snapshot, "CYP3A4|Human|Healthy")

what: Adding several building blocks at once
title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: add_*() accepts either single building block list , can attach many one call. add_*() reports many entries added.
code: patients <- list(   create_individual("Patient_A", age = 25, gender = "MALE"),   create_individual("Patient_B", age = 45, gender = "FEMALE"),   create_individual("Patient_C", age = 65, gender = "MALE") )  snapshot <- add_individual(snapshot, patients)  snapshot$individuals #>  #> ── Individuals (5) ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> • European (P-gp modified, CYP3A4 36 h) #> • Korean (Yu 2004 study) #> • Patient_A #> • Patient_B #> • Patient_C

title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: fields existing building block mutable; assign like R6 active binding. Reading object shows effect: pattern works nested objects. bump parameter individual, look path individual$parameters write value: every change, parent snapshot exports updated values: export_snapshot(snapshot, ...) writes straight back JSON, extra step required.
code: patient <- snapshot$individuals$Patient_A patient #>  #> ── Individual: Patient_A ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #>  #> ── Characteristics ── #>  #> • Gender: MALE #> • Age: 25 year(s) patient$age <- 30 patient$weight <- 75 patient$gender <- "FEMALE" patient #>  #> ── Individual: Patient_A ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #>  #> ── Characteristics ── #>  #> • Gender: FEMALE #> • Age: 30 year(s) #> • Weight: 75 kg korean <- snapshot$individuals$`Korean (Yu 2004 study)` ehc <- korean$parameters$`Organism|Liver|EHC continuous fraction` ehc #>  #> ── Parameter: Organism|Liver|EHC continuous fraction  #> • Value: 1 #> • Source: Unknown ehc$value <- 0.4 ehc #>  #> ── Parameter: Organism|Liver|EHC continuous fraction  #> • Value: 0.4 #> • Source: Unknown

title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: create_*() functions validate common inputs canonical ospsuite enums abort informative messages bad input: checks also exported standalone helpers (validate_species(), validate_gender(), validate_unit(), validate_population()) need outside create_*() call.
code: tryCatch(   {     create_individual(       name = "Invalid Patient",       species = "InvalidSpecies",       gender = "OTHER"     )   },   error = function(e) cat("Error:", e$message, "\n") ) #> Error: Invalid species: InvalidSpecies  tryCatch(   {     create_formulation(       name = "Invalid Form",       type = "NonexistentType"     )   },   error = function(e) cat("Error:", e$message, "\n") ) #> Error: subscript out of bounds

text: vignette("exporting-snapshots--dataframes") covers tibble layer analysis. full API reference https://esqlabs.github.io/osp.snapshots/reference/.

title: Exporting snapshots to data frames
text: osp.snapshots can flatten nested PK-Sim snapshot tree tibbles ready downstream analysis dplyr. vignette covers entry point, per-kind shapes, common analysis patterns.

title: Exporting snapshots to data frames
text: as_tibbles() takes snapshot plus kind naming collection convert, returns either tibble named list tibbles collections split related tables. Valid values kind: "compounds" "events" "expression_profiles" "formulations" "individuals" "observed_data" "observer_sets" "populations" "protocols" tibble layer read-: mutating returned tibble feed changes back snapshot.

title: Exporting snapshots to data frames
text: as_tibbles(snapshot, "compounds") returns list two tibbles: $properties carries one row per (compound, parameter) pair across Compound physicochemical properties. $processes long-form view: one row per (compound, process, parameter) triple across every biological Process (metabolism, transport, clearance, binding, …). $processes tibble carries category column mirrors process$category R6 side. Filtering category gives view per-category groups compound:
code: head(compound_dfs$properties) #> # A tibble: 6 × 8 #>   compound  category              type  parameter value unit  data_source source #>   <chr>     <chr>                 <chr> <chr>     <chr> <chr> <chr>       <chr>  #> 1 Midazolam physicochemical_prop… lipo… Optimized 2.89… Log … NA          Param… #> 2 Midazolam physicochemical_prop… frac… Gertz et… 0.031 NA    NA          Param… #> 3 Midazolam physicochemical_prop… mole… NA        325.… g/mol NA          NA     #> 4 Midazolam physicochemical_prop… halo… Cl        1     NA    NA          NA     #> 5 Midazolam physicochemical_prop… halo… F         1     NA    NA          NA     #> 6 Midazolam physicochemical_prop… pKa   base      6.2   NA    NA          NA head(compound_dfs$processes) #> # A tibble: 6 × 11 #>   compound  category       process_name parameter value unit  data_source source #>   <chr>     <chr>          <chr>        <chr>     <chr> <chr> <chr>       <chr>  #> 1 Midazolam protein_bindi… SpecificBin… koff      1     1/min Buhr 1997   Param… #> 2 Midazolam protein_bindi… SpecificBin… Kd        1.8   nmol… Buhr 1997   NA     #> 3 Midazolam renal_clearan… GlomerularF… GFR frac… 0.64… NA    Optimized   Param… #> 4 Midazolam metabolizing_… Metabolizat… In vitro… 850   pmol… Optimized   NA     #> 5 Midazolam metabolizing_… Metabolizat… Km        4     µmol… Optimized   aggre… #> 6 Midazolam metabolizing_… Metabolizat… kcat      8.76… 1/min Optimized   Param… #> # ℹ 3 more variables: molecule <chr>, metabolite <chr>, species <chr> compound_dfs$processes |>   filter(category == "metabolizing_enzymes")

title: Exporting snapshots to data frames
text: as_tibbles(snapshot, "individuals") returns three tibbles joinable individual_id: $individuals one row per Individual demographic fields (species, population, gender, age, weight, height, gestational age, disease state, calculation methods). $individuals_parameters long-form view individual’s parameter tree: one row per (individual, parameter path) pair, value, unit, source, value-origin metadata. $individuals_expressions lists expression profiles attached individual: one row per (individual, profile) pair.
code: individual_dfs <- as_tibbles(snapshot, "individuals") names(individual_dfs) #> [1] "individuals"             "individuals_parameters"  #> [3] "individuals_expressions"  head(individual_dfs$individuals) #> # A tibble: 2 × 18 #>   individual_id         name  description   seed species population gender   age #>   <chr>                 <chr> <chr>        <int> <chr>   <chr>      <chr>  <dbl> #> 1 European (P-gp modif… Euro… NA          1.72e7 Human   European_… MALE    30   #> 2 Korean (Yu 2004 stud… Kore… NA          5.30e7 Human   Asian_Tan… MALE    23.3 #> # ℹ 10 more variables: age_unit <chr>, gestational_age <dbl>, #> #   gestational_age_unit <chr>, weight <dbl>, weight_unit <chr>, height <dbl>, #> #   height_unit <chr>, disease_state <chr>, calculation_methods <glue>, #> #   disease_state_parameters <chr> head(individual_dfs$individuals_parameters) #> # A tibble: 2 × 7 #>   individual_id                   path  value unit  source description source_id #>   <chr>                           <chr> <dbl> <chr> <chr>  <chr>           <int> #> 1 European (P-gp modified, CYP3A… Orga…     1 NA    Unkno… NA                 NA #> 2 Korean (Yu 2004 study)          Orga…     1 NA    Unkno… NA                 NA head(individual_dfs$individuals_expressions) #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>   individual_id                         profile                                  #>   <chr>                                 <chr>                                    #> 1 European (P-gp modified, CYP3A4 36 h) CYP3A4|Human|European (P-gp modified, C… #> 2 European (P-gp modified, CYP3A4 36 h) AADAC|Human|European (P-gp modified, CY… #> 3 European (P-gp modified, CYP3A4 36 h) P-gp|Human|European (P-gp modified, CYP… #> 4 European (P-gp modified, CYP3A4 36 h) OATP1B1|Human|European (P-gp modified, … #> 5 European (P-gp modified, CYP3A4 36 h) ATP1A2|Human|European (P-gp modified, C… #> 6 European (P-gp modified, CYP3A4 36 h) UGT1A4|Human|European (P-gp modified, C…

title: Exporting snapshots to data frames
text: as_tibbles(snapshot, "formulations") returns two tibbles joinable formulation_id: $formulations one row per Formulation name release type. $formulations_parameters long-form view type-specific release parameters: one row per (formulation, parameter) pair, value unit.
code: formulation_dfs <- as_tibbles(snapshot, "formulations") names(formulation_dfs) #> [1] "formulations"            "formulations_parameters" head(formulation_dfs$formulations) #> # A tibble: 2 × 4 #>   formulation_id    name              formulation               formulation_type #>   <chr>             <chr>             <chr>                     <chr>            #> 1 Oral solution     Oral solution     Formulation_Dissolved     Dissolved        #> 2 Tablet (Dormicum) Tablet (Dormicum) Formulation_Tablet_Weibu… Weibull head(formulation_dfs$formulations_parameters) #> # A tibble: 4 × 11 #>   formulation_id    name   value unit  is_table_point x_value y_value table_name #>   <chr>             <chr>  <dbl> <chr> <lgl>            <dbl>   <dbl> <chr>      #> 1 Tablet (Dormicum) Diss… 0.0107 min   FALSE               NA      NA NA         #> 2 Tablet (Dormicum) Lag … 0      min   FALSE               NA      NA NA         #> 3 Tablet (Dormicum) Diss… 4.38   NA    FALSE               NA      NA NA         #> 4 Tablet (Dormicum) Use … 1      NA    FALSE               NA      NA NA         #> # ℹ 3 more variables: source <chr>, description <chr>, source_id <int>

title: Exporting snapshots to data frames
text: as_tibbles(snapshot, "populations") returns two tibbles joinable population_id: $populations one row per Population sampling-recipe header: source population, individual name, sample size, proportion females, seed, age, weight, height, BMI, eGFR bound columns. $populations_parameters long-form view sampling distributions PK-Sim draw : one row per (population, parameter) pair, distribution_type, statistic, value, unit.
code: population_dfs <- as_tibbles(snapshot, "populations") names(population_dfs) #> [1] "populations"            "populations_parameters" head(population_dfs$populations) #> # A tibble: 0 × 22 #> # ℹ 22 variables: population_id <chr>, name <chr>, seed <int>, #> #   number_of_individuals <int>, proportion_of_females <dbl>, #> #   source_population <chr>, individual_name <chr>, age_min <dbl>, #> #   age_max <dbl>, age_unit <chr>, weight_min <dbl>, weight_max <dbl>, #> #   weight_unit <chr>, height_min <dbl>, height_max <dbl>, height_unit <chr>, #> #   bmi_min <dbl>, bmi_max <dbl>, bmi_unit <chr>, egfr_min <dbl>, #> #   egfr_max <dbl>, egfr_unit <chr>

title: Exporting snapshots to data frames
text: as_tibbles(snapshot, "protocols") returns single 13-column tibble whether snapshot protocols . Advanced Protocols contribute one row per Schema item, protocol-level, schema-level, item-level parameters joined name.
code: protocols_df <- as_tibbles(snapshot, "protocols") head(protocols_df) #> # A tibble: 6 × 13 #>   protocol_name   schema_name schema_item_name type  formulation dosing_interval #>   <chr>           <chr>       <chr>            <chr> <chr>       <chr>           #> 1 iv 0.075 mg/kg… NA          NA               Intr… NA          Once            #> 2 iv 0.05 mg/kg … NA          NA               Intr… NA          Once            #> 3 iv 1 mg (5 min) NA          NA               Intr… NA          Once            #> 4 iv 0.001 mg (5… NA          NA               Intr… NA          Once            #> 5 iv 1 mg (bolus) NA          NA               Intr… NA          Once            #> 6 iv 2 mg (2 min) NA          NA               Intr… NA          Once            #> # ℹ 7 more variables: start_time <dbl>, start_time_unit <chr>, dose <dbl>, #> #   dose_unit <chr>, rep_number <dbl>, rep_time <chr>, rep_time_unit <chr>

title: Exporting snapshots to data frames
text: as_tibbles(snapshot, "observer_sets") returns list two tibbles joinable observer_set_id (observer_set_name): $observer_sets one row per ObserverSet name count observers contains. $observers one row per Observer name, type, dimension, formula_expression, formula_dimension, formula_references (underlying ExplicitFormula references collapsed "alias=path" pairs joined |), container_tags (|-joined Tag values underlying ContainerCriteria).

title: Exporting snapshots to data frames
text: as_tibbles(snapshot, "observed_data") flattens every observed-data series long-form tibble ready plotting.
code: obs_data_df <- as_tibbles(snapshot, "observed_data") head(obs_data_df) #> # A tibble: 6 × 30 #>   name            xValues yValues yErrorValues xDimension xUnit yDimension yUnit #>   <chr>             <dbl>   <dbl>        <dbl> <chr>      <chr> <chr>      <chr> #> 1 Ahonen 1995 - …     0.5 0.0156        0.0231 Time       h     Concentra… mg/l  #> 2 Ahonen 1995 - …     1   0.0280        0.0160 Time       h     Concentra… mg/l  #> 3 Ahonen 1995 - …     1.5 0.0235        0.0108 Time       h     Concentra… mg/l  #> 4 Ahonen 1995 - …     2   0.0185      NaN      Time       h     Concentra… mg/l  #> 5 Ahonen 1995 - …     3   0.0118      NaN      Time       h     Concentra… mg/l  #> 6 Ahonen 1995 - …     4   0.00848     NaN      Time       h     Concentra… mg/l  #> # ℹ 22 more variables: yErrorType <chr>, yErrorUnit <chr>, molWeight <dbl>, #> #   lloq <dbl>, `DB Version` <chr>, ID <chr>, `Study Id` <chr>, #> #   Reference <chr>, Source <chr>, Grouping <chr>, `Data type` <chr>, N <chr>, #> #   Molecule <chr>, Species <chr>, Organ <chr>, Compartment <chr>, Route <chr>, #> #   Dose <chr>, `Times of Administration [h]` <chr>, Formulation <chr>, #> #   `Food state` <chr>, Comment <chr>

title: Getting started with osp.snapshots

title: Building simulations from scratch
text: Every argument falls one three kinds: plain scalar, character vector names reference existing building blocks, structured object built matching create_*() sub-factory. sections follow walk sub-factory; table map. Exactly one individual population must supplied (passing , neither, errors).

what: What PK-Sim does when an optional argument is omitted
title: Building simulations from scratch
text: model: defaults "4Comp" (standard four-compartment model). description: stays empty; functional effect simulation. allow_aging: defaults FALSE load (ageing simulation window). observed_data_names: observed-data sets attached simulation. output_selections: quantities explicitly marked reporting beyond whatever model defaults . Supply least one path control simulation outputs. solver: PK-Sim builds SolverSettings ISolverSettingsFactory.CreateDefault() (built-CVODE defaults). output_schema: PK-Sim builds empty schema via IOutputSchemaFactory.CreateEmpty(); simulation reports time points. Supply least one interval get output. compounds: simulation compound configuration. Compounds referenced indirectly (e.g. via protocol) still need CompoundProperties entry runnable. events: events attached. observer_sets: observer sets attached. output_mappings: observed-data--output bindings recorded. Parameter identification downstream comparison tools nothing compare. parameters: parameter overrides applied; every quantity uses value derived building block. advanced_parameters: variability overrides population sampling; default distributions apply.

title: Building simulations from scratch
text: create_solver_settings() produces sparse solver configuration. arguments optional, PK-Sim fallback omitted: abs_tol, rel_tol, use_jacobian, h0, h_min, h_max, mx_step: field omitted snapshot filled ISolverSettingsFactory.CreateDefault() load, simulation runs PK-Sim’s built-CVODE defaults. Calling create_solver_settings() arguments supplying solver create_simulation() .

title: Building simulations from scratch
text: create_output_schema() collects one Output intervals. Required: none. intervals: defaults empty list. output schema intervals means simulation reports time points; supply least one interval want output. create_output_interval(): Required: start_time, end_time, resolution, numeric scalars PK-Sim’s default units (h, h, pts/h). name: PK-Sim assigns generated unique name IContainerTask.CreateUniqueName().
code: schema <- create_output_schema(   intervals = list(     create_output_interval(start_time = 0, end_time = 2, resolution = 20),     create_output_interval(start_time = 2, end_time = 24, resolution = 20)   ) )

title: Building simulations from scratch
Building simulations from scratch
text: Compound building block (kind find snapshot$compounds) project-level definition drug: physicochemical properties, full menu available alternatives, process templates ships, . compound can referenced many simulations. CompoundProperties per-simulation overlay picks one path menu. simulation uses compound record: calculation_methods override compound’s defaults, alternatives (e.g. solubility, permeability) selected, processes enabled, wired molecules systemic types, protocol used formulation fills application slot. None choices live Compound building block, create_compound_properties() exists separate step even compound already snapshot$compounds. reference back building block name argument; everything else simulation-local. contrast, create_event_selection() takes name plus start_time, create_observer_set_selection() takes name, events observer sets nothing analogous alternatives/processes/protocol choose simulation time.

title: Building simulations from scratch
text: Required: name, must match compound snapshot. calculation_methods: simulation uses calculation-method cache already defined Compound building block. alternatives: unspecified alternative group keeps default alternative marked compound (typically first one IsDefault = TRUE). processes: every compound process listed treated deselected; PK-Sim materialises right placeholder (NotSelectedSystemicProcess, NoInteractionProcess, …) simulation runs explicitly disabled . protocol: omitted, compound included dosing protocol attached, means administered simulation (useful compounds produced metabolically). supporting factories follow convention: create_compound_process_selection(): every field optional; PK-Sim resolves right placeholder whichever combination name, molecule_name, metabolite_name, compound_name, systemic_process_type supplied. reference building block; name field process name internal parent compound. create_compound_group_selection(): group_name alternative_name required. reference building block; picks alternative inside alternative group defined parent compound.
code: compound <- create_compound_properties(   name = "Midazolam",   calculation_methods = c(     "Cellular partition coefficient method - Rodgers and Rowland",     "Cellular permeability - PK-Sim Standard"   ),   processes = list(     create_compound_process_selection(systemic_process_type = "Hepatic")   ),   protocol = create_protocol_selection(     name = "iv 1 mg (bolus)",     formulations = list(       create_formulation_selection(         name = "Oral solution",         key = "Formulation"       )     )   ) )

title: Building simulations from scratch
text: create_protocol_selection() mirrors split compounds. Protocol building block (snapshot$protocols) project-level dosing schedule parameters. ProtocolSelection per-simulation overlay points one protocol name records Formulation fills protocol’s application slots formulations (list create_formulation_selection() outputs). Required: name (must match protocol snapshot). formulations: protocol application slots formulation binding stay empty; PK-Sim reject simulation load time protocol requires formulation unbound slot. Supply formulations every slot needs one (typically every slot oral protocols).

title: Building simulations from scratch
text: idea . Formulation building block (snapshot$formulations) project-level dissolution profile. FormulationSelection per-simulation entry binds formulation one application slot inside chosen protocol. Required: name (must match formulation snapshot) key (application slot identifier inside protocol).

title: Building simulations from scratch
text: Event building block (snapshot$events) project-level event definition (template plus parameters). EventSelection per-simulation reference picks one event name supplies start_time simulation. Aside start_time, events carry per-simulation overlay. create_event_selection(): Required: name (must match event building block) start_time (numeric, hours).

title: Building simulations from scratch
text: ObserverSet building block (snapshot$observer_sets) project-level observer bundle. ObserverSetSelection per-simulation reference picks one observer set name. per-simulation overlay , factory takes single argument. create_observer_set_selection(): Required: name (must match observer-set building block). Midazolam template ships observer sets, skip create_observer_set_selection() walkthrough. template one (snapshot$observer_sets), call look like:
code: event <- create_event_selection(name = "High-fat breakfast", start_time = 12) observer_set <- create_observer_set_selection(   name = "BrainPlasmaConcentration" )

title: Building simulations from scratch
text: Simulation parameter overrides localized parameters, identified full container path inside simulation’s parameter tree. Use create_parameter(path = ...) obtain one.
code: overrides <- list(   create_parameter(     path = "Organism|Liver|Volume",     value = 1.8,     unit = "L"   ) )

title: Building simulations from scratch
text: every input prepared, final create_simulation() call wires together.
code: sim <- create_simulation(   name = "Midazolam single dose",   model = "4Comp",   individual = "Korean (Yu 2004 study)",   compounds = list(compound),   events = list(event),   solver = solver,   output_schema = schema,   output_selections = c(     "Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Midazolam|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)"   ),   parameters = overrides )  sim #>  #> ── Simulation: Midazolam single dose ─────────────────────────────────────────── #> • Model: 4Comp #> • Individual: Korean (Yu 2004 study) #>  #> ── Compounds (1) ── #>  #> • Midazolam #>  #> ── Events (1) ── #>  #> • High-fat breakfast #>  #> ── Output selections (1) ── #>  #> • Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Midazolam|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood) #>  #> ── Output schema ── #>  #> • 2 intervals #>  #> ── Solver ── #>  #> • AbsTol: 1e-09 #> • RelTol: 1e-09 #> • MxStep: 100000 #>  #> ── Parameter overrides (1) ──

Convert a snapshot collection to a tibble or list of tibbles — as_tibbles

title: Convert a snapshot collection to a tibble or list of tibbles — as_tibbles
text: tibble named list tibbles, depending kind: "compounds": list properties processes tibbles. properties carries one row per (compound, physicochemical property) pair (plus folded process rows backwards compatibility); processes long-form, one row per (compound, process, parameter) triple. "protocols", "observed_data": single tibble. "observer_sets": list observer_sets (one row per ObserverSet) observers (one row per Observer, joinable parent via observer_set_id / observer_set_name). "individuals": list individuals, individuals_parameters, individuals_expressions. "formulations": list formulations, formulations_parameters. "populations": list populations, populations_parameters. "events": list events, events_parameters. "expression_profiles": list expression_profiles, expression_profiles_parameters.

Create a new compound — create_compound
text: Create Compound building block named arguments. thin factory around Compound$new() builds raw list shape , including physicochemical properties, pKa, processes. lipophilicity, fraction unbound, solubility (including reference pH, gain per charge, table solubility), intestinal permeability, permeability, pKa must set dedicated argument (mutating matching writable Compound field). parameters argument additional/loose compound parameters ; set physicochemical properties, lives top-level alternative array typed list rather Parameters.

title: Create a new compound — create_compound
code: create_compound(   name,   description = NULL,   is_small_molecule = NULL,   plasma_protein_binding_partner = NULL,   molecular_weight = NULL,   molecular_weight_unit = "g/mol",   calculation_methods = NULL,   parameters = NULL,   lipophilicity = NULL,   lipophilicity_unit = "Log Units",   lipophilicity_name = "User defined",   fraction_unbound = NULL,   fraction_unbound_name = "User defined",   solubility = NULL,   solubility_unit = "mg/l",   reference_pH = NULL,   solubility_gain_per_charge = NULL,   solubility_table = NULL,   solubility_name = "User defined",   intestinal_permeability = NULL,   intestinal_permeability_unit = "cm/min",   intestinal_permeability_name = "User defined",   permeability = NULL,   permeability_unit = "cm/min",   permeability_name = "User defined",   pKa = NULL,   processes = NULL )

title: Create a new compound — create_compound
text: name Character. Name compound (required). description Character. Free-text description compound. is_small_molecule Logical. Whether compound small molecule. Defaults TRUE PK-Sim omitted. plasma_protein_binding_partner Character. Name plasma protein binding partner (example "Albumin"). molecular_weight Numeric. Molecular weight value. molecular_weight_unit Character. Unit molecular weight. Defaults "g/mol". calculation_methods Character vector. Calculation method names PK-Sim uses derive compound quantities. parameters List Parameter objects (created create_parameter()) raw parameter lists attach additional compound parameters. set physicochemical properties; use dedicated arguments . lipophilicity Numeric scalar. Lipophilicity value. supplied, one default Lipophilicity alternative created. lipophilicity_unit Character. Unit lipophilicity parameter, validated dimension "Log Units". Defaults "Log Units". lipophilicity_name Character. Name created lipophilicity alternative. Defaults "User defined". fraction_unbound Numeric scalar. Fraction unbound value. supplied, one default FractionUnbound alternative created. fraction-unbound parameter carries unit. fraction_unbound_name Character. Name created fraction-unbound alternative. Defaults "User defined". solubility Numeric scalar. Solubility--reference-pH value. supplied, one scalar-based Solubility alternative created. Mutually exclusive solubility_table. solubility_unit Character. Unit solubility parameter, validated dimension "Concentration (mass)". Reused table Y unit. Defaults "mg/l". reference_pH Numeric scalar. Reference pH added scalar Solubility alternative solubility. standalone property: ignored unless solubility supplied, apply table path. solubility_gain_per_charge Numeric scalar. Optional Solubility gain per charge parameter added scalar Solubility alternative solubility. standalone property: ignored unless solubility supplied. solubility_table Two-column data frame giving table-based solubility: first column pH second solubility value. supplied, one Solubility alternative Solubility table parameter carrying TableFormula created instead scalar solubility. Mutually exclusive solubility. Note: import PK-Sim runs table-solubility preparation step package perform; package emits faithful raw Solubility table + TableFormula shape round-trips load/export JSON level. solubility_name Character. Name created solubility alternative (scalar table). Defaults "User defined". intestinal_permeability Numeric scalar. Intestinal permeability value. supplied, one default IntestinalPermeability alternative created. intestinal_permeability_unit Character. Unit intestinal-permeability parameter, validated dimension "Velocity". Defaults "cm/min". intestinal_permeability_name Character. Name created intestinal-permeability alternative. Defaults "User defined". permeability Numeric scalar. Permeability value. supplied, one default Permeability alternative created. permeability_unit Character. Unit permeability parameter, validated dimension "Velocity". Defaults "cm/min". permeability_name Character. Name created permeability alternative. Defaults "User defined". pKa List typed pKa entries, list type (one "Acid", "Base", "Neutral") numeric value, example list(list(type = "Base", value = 10.02)). Order preserved. NULL (default) empty list() leave created compound pKa types set. (clearing semantics, empty list() removes existing values, apply writable Compound$pka_types field, construction.) processes List Process objects (created create_process()) raw process lists attach compound.

title: Create a new compound — create_compound
Create a new compound — create_compound
code: # Create a minimal compound compound <- create_compound(name = "Drug X")  # Create a small molecule with molecular weight and binding partner compound <- create_compound(   name = "Drug X",   is_small_molecule = TRUE,   molecular_weight = 250.3,   plasma_protein_binding_partner = "Albumin" )  # Set the single-parameter physicochemical properties compound <- create_compound(   name = "Drug X",   lipophilicity = 2.5,   fraction_unbound = 0.1,   intestinal_permeability = 1.14e-05,   permeability = 0.0069 )  # Solubility with reference pH and gain per charge compound <- create_compound(   name = "Drug X",   solubility = 9999,   reference_pH = 7,   solubility_gain_per_charge = 1000 )  # Table-based solubility (first column pH, second column value) compound <- create_compound(   name = "Drug X",   solubility_table = data.frame(     pH = c(3, 6, 6.8),     value = c(5000, 3000, 90)   ) )  # Multiple pKa entries compound <- create_compound(   name = "Drug X",   pKa = list(     list(type = "Base", value = 10.02),     list(type = "Acid", value = 1.7)   ) )  # Attach a process compound <- create_compound(   name = "Drug X",   processes = list(     create_process(       internal_name = "GlomerularFiltration",       data_source = "Publication X"     )   ) )  # Additional/loose parameters (not physicochemical properties) compound <- create_compound(   name = "Drug X",   parameters = list(     create_parameter(name = "Cl_spec", value = 5, unit = "ml/min/kg")   ) )

title: Create a new formulation — create_formulation
text: thickness - Thickness unstirred water layer (numeric, default: 30) thickness_unit - Unit thickness (character, default: "\u00b5m") distribution_type - Type distribution, "mono" "poly" (character, default: "mono") radius - Particle radius (mean geometric mean) (numeric, default: 10) radius_unit - Unit radius (character, default: "\u00b5m") Parameters polydisperse distribution (distribution_type = "poly"): particle_size_distribution - "normal" "lognormal" (character, default: "normal") radius_sd - Radius standard deviation, normal distribution (numeric, default: 3) radius_sd_unit - Unit radius SD (character, default: "\u00b5m") radius_cv - Coefficient variation, lognormal distribution (numeric, default: 1.5) radius_min - Minimum particle radius (numeric, default: 1) radius_min_unit - Unit minimum radius (character, default: "\u00b5m") radius_max - Maximum particle radius (numeric, default: 19) radius_max_unit - Unit maximum radius (character, default: "\u00b5m") n_bins - Number bins (integer, default: 3)

title: Create a new population — create_population
code: create_population(   name,   number_of_individuals,   proportion_of_females = 50,   individual_name = NULL,   species = NULL,   source_population = NULL,   age_range = NULL,   weight_range = NULL,   height_range = NULL,   bmi_range = NULL,   seed = NULL,   advanced_parameters = NULL,   description = NULL,   gestational_age_range = NULL,   disease_state_parameters = NULL,   individual = NULL )

title: Create a new population — create_population
text: name Character. Name population (required). number_of_individuals Integer. Number subjects generate (required, must positive integer). proportion_of_females Numeric. Percentage females population, 0 100. Defaults 50. individual_name Character. Name base Individual building block population samples . species Character. Species used base individual individual_name provided. source_population Character. Population name used base individual (example "European_ICRP_2002"). age_range Range object describing age bounds (see range()). weight_range Range object describing weight bounds. height_range Range object describing height bounds. bmi_range Range object describing BMI bounds. seed Integer. Random seed population generation. advanced_parameters List AdvancedParameter objects raw advanced-parameter lists override default variability distributions. description Character. Free-text description population. gestational_age_range Range object describing gestational age range used population generation (see range()). population-settings range, distinct base individual's scalar gestational age. disease_state_parameters named list mapping disease-state parameter name Range object, example list(eGFR = range(60, 120, "ml/min/1.73m²")). entry becomes one range population settings. population-level name--Range map distinct base individual's disease_state_parameters, carries scalar parameter values. individual Individual object (typically create_individual()) used base individual population samples . supplied, fully configures base individual (gender, origin data, calculation methods, seed, description, parameters) combined individual_name, species, source_population.

title: Create a new population — create_population
Create a new population — create_population
code: # Create a minimal population pop <- create_population(   name = "Adults",   number_of_individuals = 100 )  # Create a population with demographic ranges pop <- create_population(   name = "Healthy Adults",   number_of_individuals = 50,   proportion_of_females = 50,   species = "Human",   source_population = "European_ICRP_2002",   age_range = range(20, 60, "year(s)"),   weight_range = range(50, 90, "kg") )  # Compose a base individual and set a disease-state parameter pop <- create_population(   name = "Renal Impairment",   number_of_individuals = 25,   individual = create_individual(     name = "Base",     species = "Human",     population = "European_ICRP_2002",     gender = "MALE"   ),   gestational_age_range = range(37, 42, "week(s)"),   disease_state_parameters = list(     eGFR = range(60, 120, "ml/min/1.73m²")   ),   description = "Adults with reduced renal function" )

title: Create a new protocol — create_protocol
text: name Character. Name protocol (required). application_type Character. Application type Simple Protocol (example "Oral", "IntravenousBolus", "IntravenousInfusion"). Mutually exclusive schemas. dosing_interval Character. Dosing interval identifier Simple Protocol (example "Single", "DI_12_12", "DI_8_8_8", "DI_24"). target_organ Character. Target organ dose. target_compartment Character. Target compartment dose. parameters List Parameter objects (created create_parameter()) raw parameter lists Simple Protocol parameters start time, end time, dose. schemas List schemas Advanced Protocol. Entries may Schema objects (created create_schema()) raw schema lists Name, Parameters, SchemaItems. provided, protocol created Advanced Protocol. time_unit Character. Display time unit protocol.

title: Create a new protocol — create_protocol
Create a new protocol — create_protocol
code: # Create a simple oral protocol with one dose protocol <- create_protocol(   name = "Single dose 10mg",   application_type = "Oral",   dosing_interval = "Single",   parameters = list(     create_parameter(name = "Start time", value = 0, unit = "h"),     create_parameter(name = "InputDose", value = 10, unit = "mg")   ) )  # Create a twice-daily intravenous protocol protocol <- create_protocol(   name = "BID IV",   application_type = "IntravenousBolus",   dosing_interval = "DI_12_12",   parameters = list(     create_parameter(name = "InputDose", value = 5, unit = "mg")   ) )  # Create an Advanced Protocol from Schema objects protocol <- create_protocol(   name = "Advanced",   schemas = list(     create_schema(       name = "Schema 1",       parameters = list(         create_parameter(name = "NumberOfRepetitions", value = 1)       ),       items = list(         create_schema_item(           name = "Item 1",           application_type = "Oral",           parameters = list(             create_parameter(name = "InputDose", value = 5, unit = "mg")           )         )       )     )   ),   time_unit = "h" )

osp.snapshots 1.0.0.9000
text: create_population() gains description, gestational_age_range, disease_state_parameters, individual arguments, last composing base individual create_individual() fully configure population’s base individual (#118). Population objects gain read/write description, gestational_age_range, disease_state_parameters bindings read-individual binding exposing base individual (#118). Population$egfr_range now persists population settings, eGFR range set population survives export (#118).

title: osp.snapshots 1.0.0
text: compound$calculation_methods returns CalculationMethods R6 object instead plain list class compound_calculation_methods. Read names compound$calculation_methods$names mutate $add(name), $remove(name); $length reports count (#30). compound$processes returns flat named list Process R6 objects (duplicate names disambiguated _{n}). Filter process$category recover equivalent deprecated per-category accessors (#40). create_parameter() writes identifier data$Name plain parameters (path argument) data$Path path-bearing parameters (path argument). Previously every result carried data$Path. Pass path = ... get path-bearing parameter; pass name = ... get plain one (#52). get_compounds_dfs() returns list two tibbles, properties processes, instead single combined tibble. Update callers df <- get_compounds_dfs(snap) dfs <- get_compounds_dfs(snap); df <- dfs$properties, switch new long-form dfs$processes (#40). load_snapshot() now requires PK-Sim v11.2 snapshots newer (Version >= 79). Re-export older projects PK-Sim v11.2+ loading (#52).

title: osp.snapshots 1.0.0
text: add_simulation() attaches one Simulation objects Snapshot. Unresolved references building blocks (individual, population, compounds, events, observer sets, observed data, protocols, formulations) trigger one informational warning per simulation; add proceeds either way (#94). add_*() mutators now accept either single building block list building blocks, mirroring remove_*() accepted character vector names since #66. Success messages sides now uniformly report Added N kind(s) / Removed N kind(s) (#92). as_tibbles(snapshot, kind) converts building-block collection tibble one entry point, returning either bare tibble ("protocols", "observed_data") named list related tibbles (every kind). eight existing get_*_dfs() functions remain available thin wrappers (#36). compound$calculation_methods individual$origin_data$calculation_methods return CalculationMethods object can inspect ($names, $length) mutate ($add(), $remove()) (#30). compound$processes returns named list Process objects, exposing internal_name, data_source, molecule, metabolite, species, parameters, derived category (#40). create_compound() builds compound named arguments, validation molecular_weight_unit ospsuite::ospUnits$"Molecular weight" (#27, #48). also sets physicochemical properties directly: lipophilicity, fraction_unbound, solubility (reference_pH, solubility_gain_per_charge, solubility_table), intestinal_permeability, permeability, pKa, attaches processes; matching Compound fields ($lipophilicity, $fraction_unbound, $solubility, $intestinal_permeability, $permeability, $pka_types, $processes) writable loaded compound can mutated. Attach add_compound(), remove name remove_compound() (#39, #115). create_compound_group_selection(), create_compound_process_selection(), create_compound_properties(), create_event_selection(), create_formulation_selection(), create_observer_set_selection(), create_output_interval(), create_output_mapping(), create_output_schema(), create_protocol_selection(), create_simulation(), create_solver_settings() build Simulation supporting structures named arguments (#94). create_event() builds event template name optional list parameter overrides (#27). Attach add_event(), remove remove_event() (#39). create_expression_profile() builds expression profile, requiring molecule, species, category, type (#27). create_individual() gains expression_profiles, description, parameters arguments attach expression-profile references, description, localized parameter overrides creation time; Individual$expression_profiles binding now writable new read/write Individual$description binding available (#117). create_observed_data() builds ospsuite::DataSet named time, value, unit, optional error-series arguments. value_dimension required gates unit validation (#27, #48). Attach add_observed_data(), remove remove_observed_data() (#39). create_observer_set() builds observer set name list observers (#43). Attach add_observer_set(), remove remove_observer_set() (#38). create_population() builds population, taking Range objects age, weight, height, BMI bounds. number_of_individuals must positive integer; proportion_of_females must length-1 number (#27, #48). Attach add_population() (#39). create_process() builds compound process; internal_name data_source validated (#40). See ?create_process list supported internal_name values. create_protocol() builds Simple Advanced protocol. Pass schemas build Advanced protocol (accepts Schema objects create_schema() raw lists); mixing Simple-fields (application_type, dosing_interval, …) schemas errors (#27, #48, #54). Attach add_protocol(), remove remove_protocol() (#39). create_schema() create_schema_item() build repeatable blocks individual applications used inside Advanced protocol. application_type schema item validated canonical PK-Sim application types (#54). create_snapshot() creates empty snapshot carrying current supported PK-Sim version, optionally named described, snapshot-level counterpart load_snapshot() export_snapshot() (#112). individual$origin_data returns OriginData object holding demographic starting point individual (species, population, gender, age, weight, height, gestational age, calculation methods, optional disease state) (#30). ObserverSet objects now fully supported end end (load, mutate, convert, export). exposes observers named list Observer objects name, type, dimension, formula (full ExplicitFormula list), formula_expression, formula_dimension, formula_references, container_tags. get_observer_sets_dfs() returns two tibbles (observer_sets set-level rows, observers joinable back via observer_set_id / observer_set_name); observers tibble carries formula_expression, formula_dimension, formula_references columns alongside name, type, dimension, container_tags (#38, #42, #76, #79). protocol$schemas returns named list Schema objects, exposing $items list SchemaItem objects (application type, formulation key, target organ compartment, parameters) (#29). remove_simulation() removes one simulations Snapshot name (#94). Simulation now first-class building block wrapping simulation slice snapshot R6 accessors solver settings, output schemas, compound configurations, event observer-set selections, observed-data references, output mappings, LocalizedParameter overrides. Four post-run fields (Interactions, AlteredBuildingBlocks, IndividualAnalyses, PopulationAnalyses) preserved byte-equivalent $data, construction enforces XOR $individual $population exactly one subject configured (#94). Parameters live parameter tree (Individual, ExpressionProfile, Simulation) now LocalizedParameter objects, identified pipe-separated path. Pre-v11 Applications|... segments migrated Events|... load. create_parameter() routes LocalizedParameter called path argument (#31).

title: osp.snapshots 1.0.0
text: as_tibbles(snapshot, "protocols") (legacy get_protocols_dfs() wrapper) now returns 13 columns whether snapshot protocols . Previously empty-state path returned 18-column tibble disagreed populated path, breaking bind_rows() across mixed snapshots (#56). create_compound() output can now printed is_small_molecule left unset. Previously printing compound aborted “missing value TRUE/FALSE needed”; type line now omitted instead (#115). export_snapshot() now serializes DataSet objects attached runtime via add_observed_data(), snapshot built create_observed_data() add_observed_data() round-trips export_snapshot() load_snapshot(). Entries already present loaded snapshot still replayed original JSON slice, means post-load mutations DataSet (e.g. changing xUnit entry snapshot$observed_data) reflected export (#35, #96). loadDataSetFromSnapshot() now preserves observed-data time unit (BaseGrid$Unit) resulting DataSet$xUnit every ospsuite Time unit, including "day(s)", "week(s)", "month(s)", "year(s)", "ks". Previously "h", "min", "s" survived unit silently reverted "h", misplacing time points (example 24x error day(s)); also affected create_observed_data(time_unit = ...) (#104). remove_expression_profile(), remove_formulation(), remove_individual(), remove_observed_data(), remove_population() now report actual number entries removed instead length input vector, success message reads correctly requested name present snapshot (#66). Building-block collections now share snapshot_collection S3 class single generic print() method. existing per-kind classes (compound_collection, individual_collection, …) preserved marker classes (#34). Observed data removed via remove_observed_data() now dropped exported snapshot. Previously export reused full original ObservedData list whenever lazy cache touched, re-introducing removed entries round-trip. fix applies every building-block section: clearing collection via remove_individual(), remove_formulation(), remove_population(), remove_expression_profile() now writes empty section export instead falling back original entries (#35, #59). Single-element JSON arrays remain arrays export/import, allowing exported snapshots load PK-Sim (#23). eight category-keyed compound accessors ($protein_binding_partners, $metabolizing_enzymes, $hepatic_clearance, $transporter_proteins, $renal_clearance, $biliary_clearance, $inhibition, $induction) soft-deprecated. Use compound$processes (filter process$category) long-form processes tibble returned get_compounds_dfs() (#40).

New data frame conversion functions building block types: get_individuals_dfs(), get_compounds_dfs(), get_formulations_dfs(), get_populations_dfs(), get_protocols_dfs(), get_events_dfs(), get_expression_profiles_dfs(), get_observed_data_dfs(). New creation functions: create_individual(), create_formulation(), create_parameter() building new building blocks. New management functions: add_individual(), add_formulation(), add_expression_profile(), remove_individual(), remove_formulation(), remove_population(), remove_expression_profile(). Full integration ospsuite::DataSet objects observed data. Comprehensive input validation species, populations, genders, units. Time conversion utilities OSPSuite R lubridate formats. Structured print methods building block classes.
